description:
A security engineer has asked you to perform a **credential-rotation** procedure for a small command-line application that stores its database credentials in a “dotenv” file.

    Initial system state (already present before you start):
      • Directory: /home/user/app  
      • File: /home/user/app/.env  
        └─ Content (exactly two lines, Unix line-endings):
           APP_DB_USER=olduser
           APP_DB_PASS=oldpass123

    Your job, step-by-step:

    1. Create a **backup** named “.env.bak” in the same directory.  
       • Full path: /home/user/app/.env.bak  
       • The backup file must be an exact, byte-for-byte copy of the original .env before you touch it.

    2. Overwrite /home/user/app/.env with the **new credentials** below (exactly two lines, no extra spaces, Unix line-endings):
           APP_DB_USER=newuser2023
           APP_DB_PASS=newpass!56

    3. Export the new values into the **current shell environment** so that, for the remainder of the session,  
       the commands `echo $APP_DB_USER` and `echo $APP_DB_PASS` would output “newuser2023” and “newpass!56” respectively.

    4. Produce an **audit log** so future engineers can verify what happened.  
       • Create the directory /home/user/rotation_logs if it does not already exist.  
       • Create/overwrite the file /home/user/rotation_logs/credential_rotation.log with _exactly_ the six lines shown below, in this order, with a trailing newline after each line (again, Unix LF line-endings):
           OLD_USER=olduser
           OLD_PASS=oldpass123
           NEW_USER=newuser2023
           NEW_PASS=newpass!56
           STATUS=SUCCESS

       *Note:* “credential_rotation.log” must **not** contain timestamps or any other text; only the six lines above.

    5. Make sure all new/modified files are world-readable (mode 0644) and owned by the current unprivileged user.

    Verification hints (what the automated grader will check):
      • /home/user/app/.env.bak exists and is identical to the original .env file.  
      • /home/user/app/.env now contains the new credentials and **only** those two lines.  
      • /home/user/rotation_logs/credential_rotation.log exists and its content is byte-for-byte identical to the six-line block shown above.  
      • File permissions for the three files are 0644 (rw-r--r--).  
      • The directory /home/user/rotation_logs exists.  
      • Optional: Environment variables may be inspected, but they are not part of the strict automated check, so feel free to print them to the console for human visibility.

    Follow the steps in order, and remember to keep the output format of “credential_rotation.log” **exactly** as specified.
truth:
<!--
        Ground truth for automated tests.
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/app/.env">APP_DB_USER=olduser
APP_DB_PASS=oldpass123
</file>
    </initial_files>

    <files_after_completion>
        <file path="/home/user/app/.env.bak" perms="0644" owner="user">APP_DB_USER=olduser
APP_DB_PASS=oldpass123
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/app/.env" perms="0644" owner="user">APP_DB_USER=newuser2023
APP_DB_PASS=newpass!56
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/rotation_logs/credential_rotation.log" perms="0644" owner="user">OLD_USER=olduser
OLD_PASS=oldpass123
NEW_USER=newuser2023
NEW_PASS=newpass!56
STATUS=SUCCESS
        </file>
    </files_after_completion>
